exemplar.title:
How does noise pollution vary between Galaxy shopping centre and
Kasprowicza Park in the city during various hours?

detailedExemplarData.title:
How does noise pollution vary between Galaxy shopping centre and
Kasprowicza Park in the city during various hours?

subject.title: Geography
subject.description:
**IB Geography** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **physical and human geography, environmental interactions, global challenges, and spatial patterns**, with a strong focus on **fieldwork, data analysis, case studies, and Geographic Information Systems (GIS)**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Geographic themes - structured questions | 2h 15min | 2h 15min | SL: 35% / HL: 35% |
| **Paper 2** | Geographic perspectives - data response and essays | 1h 15min | 1h 15min | SL: 40% / HL: 45% |
| **Paper 3** | Global interactions - extended essay responses | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Fieldwork investigation (2,500 words) | 20 hours | 20 hours | SL: 25% / HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear, testable geographical question with directional hypotheses linked to syllabus theory
Precise geographic context with two wellâjustified urban sites in Szczecin and feasibility for primary data collection
Explicit link to Option G Urban Environments and ability to form hypotheses
weaknesses: Some map conventions are inconsistently applied (obscuring symbols, missing legends)
Locational precision could be enhanced with grid references
Research question could specify exact time blocks for greater precision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place two elements at top band and one at mid band. The question and context are exemplary, meeting highest descriptors, while mapping is strong but marred by convention errors. A full 3 marks reflect excellent overall performance with minor mapping refinements needed.
feedback.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

strengths: Systematic recording of decibel readings at regular intervals demonstrating reproducibility
Clear description of methods that align directly to the research question
weaknesses: Lacks detailed sampling design (replicate counts, calibration procedures)
Uncertainty over device accuracy and confined to singleâday sampling reduces reliability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Methods are adequately described and yield usable data, matching midâband performance (Band 2) for both relevance and data quality. A score of 2 reflects clear execution but limited methodological depth and reliability.

strengths: All primary data are directly relevant and sufficient for partial analysis
Appropriate presentation methods (tables, bar graphs) with clear labels
Conventions (titles, scale bars, north arrows) mostly adhered to
weaknesses: Dataset limited in temporal scope and site number
Minor convention errors (mixed decimal separators, missing axis units)
Lack of more effective visualization techniques (e.g., line graphs, spatial overlays)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data relevance and treatment consistently meet the 3â4 band descriptors but show clear limitations in scope and some presentation errors. A conservative score of 3 acknowledges solid presentation while noting need for greater data robustness and flawless conventions.
feedback.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

strengths: Appropriate descriptive analysis linking averages and trends to hypotheses
Identification of trends and anomalies with reference to urban stress theory
Coherent discussion covering all data strands
weaknesses: No statistical tests or measures of variability included
Limited depth in exploring anomalies and theoretical integration
Evidence gaps remain around measurement error and intraâsite variation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Analysis aligns with midâband performance (Band 3â4) by using suitable descriptive techniques and linking to theory, but lacks comprehensive statistical treatment and deeper theoretical exploration. A score of 3 reflects solid descriptive work with scope for enhanced analytical rigor.

strengths: Clear, direct conclusion that fully links findings to hypotheses
Conclusion grounded in analysis and connected to urban planning implications
weaknesses: Could be strengthened by quantifying noise reduction against guidelines
Limited discussion of broader implications beyond initial findings
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is fully supported by the analysis and meets the topâband descriptor for Criterion E, warranting 2 marks; minor enhancements would add actionable context.

strengths: Identification of relevant strengths and weaknesses demonstrating reflective insight
Practical improvement suggestions (professional equipment, extended sampling) outlined
weaknesses: Lacks inâdepth analysis of how each limitation biases results
Improvement proposals are not detailed in impact or implementation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation outlines appropriate strengths/weaknesses and offers relevant suggestions, consistent with midâband performance (Band 2). A score of 2 reflects sound, but not comprehensive, evaluation and improvement planning.

strandSelections.reasoning: Several maps are included and north arrows / scale bars appear on main maps.  Nonetheless, circles and large red arrows obscure features and some captions are vague; one ânoise mapâ is a screenshot with excessive detail.  Overall mapping is useful but not consistently following best cartographic conventions, matching the mid-band descriptor.

The student frames a clear, geographical question focusing on spatial variation in noise pollution between two contrasting urban sites.  The link to Option G Urban Environments is explicitly stated and two directional hypotheses are provided, demonstrating that the question can be tested.  This aligns with the top-band descriptor that stresses clear theoretical links and the possibility of hypothesis formation.

Tables and simple bar graphs are appropriate and clearly labelled, enabling comparison between time periods and locations.  However, there is little variety (e.g., no line graphs or spatial overlays) and axes lack units on some images, so the work sits solidly in the âappropriateâ rather than âmost effectiveâ category (score 3).

Data were collected correctly with regular intervals and transferred to spreadsheets, producing usable primary data.  However, measurements were confined to a single day per site and device accuracy is uncertain, so reliability is moderate rather than âhigh-qualityâ; this corresponds to band 2.

The student uses appropriate descriptive techniques (comparative commentary, reference to averages, maxima, minima) but no statistical tests.  That matches the 3-4 band; with no evident statistical error analysis, the lower mark of 3 is merited.

Trends between sites and across times are clearly linked to hypotheses and syllabus ideas (urban stresses).  Anomalies are briefly noted (evening noise still high at mall) but not deeply explored.  This fits the middle band, warranting 3 marks.

Specific sites (Galaxy shopping centre and Kasprowicza Park) within Szczecin are named, described and justified; feasibility for primary data collection is obvious.  Background locational detail (city, voivodeship) is supplied, satisfying the requirement for a precise context.

The evaluation outlines several relevant strengths and weaknesses (device accuracy, weather, temporal coverage) but does not probe their impact in depth.  This aligns with the mid-band descriptor for evaluation depth.

All recorded data (min, max, average dB for three periods at two sites) are directly relevant to answering the question, but the dataset is limited in temporal scope and number of sites.  This allows partial but not exhaustive analysis, aligning with the 3â4 band; a conservative score of 3 is appropriate.

A clear, direct conclusion restates findings and links back to both hypotheses with supporting evidence from the analysis.  It is therefore âfully supportedâ, satisfying the top band for Criterion E (2 marks).

All data strands (tables, graphs) are referenced and discussed, giving a coherent answer, yet analysis ignores measurement error and spatial variation within each site.  Evidence gaps remain but the analysis is more than minimal, so a band-3 score is appropriate.

Methods (phone/computer sound-meter, five-minute interval recordings, three time blocks) are described and clearly relate to the research question, yet sampling design (exact points, number of replicates, equipment calibration) is only briefly noted.  Explanation is adequate but not fully comprehensive, placing it in band 2.

Most tables, graphs and maps include titles; north arrows and scale bars are present on core maps.  Minor convention errors (comma instead of decimal point, duplicated graph titles) occur but do not impede understanding.  Occasional rather than frequent errors justify a band-3 score.

Improvement suggestionsâuse professional equipment, sample over more days, add more sitesâare relevant but lack detailed explanation of how each change would influence results.  The work therefore fits the 2-mark band for suggestions.

annotations.comment: Citing quantitative values or statistics from the existing noise maps (Maps 3â5) when describing background context would strengthen the justification using secondary data.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

annotations.comment: Map 1 effectively locates Szczecin within Poland, following basic cartographic conventions and orienting the reader to the broader geographic context.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

annotations.comment: Map 2âs large circle and red arrow obscure underlying features, and the caption omits scale and north arrow details; refine symbology and include full cartographic conventions.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

annotations.comment: The title clearly specifies a fieldwork focus on noise pollution in Szczecin, effectively setting the scene and aligning with Option G Urban Environments.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

annotations.comment: While the research question is clear, refining it to specify the exact time blocks (e.g., morning, noon, night) within the question itself would increase precision and testability.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

annotations.comment: The detailed table of contents demonstrates excellent organization and logical sequencing of sections, aiding reader navigation.
The geographical background succinctly describes two contrasting sites within Szczecin, justifying their selection and establishing clear feasibility for primary data collection.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

annotations.comment: The description of Galaxy mall would benefit from inclusion of precise coordinates or grid references to enhance locational accuracy and improve spatial context.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

annotations.comment: The discussion of nighttime park quietness effectively highlights environmental function and ties back to syllabus concepts on urban stress mitigation.
annotations.groupName: Criterion D: Written Analysis

annotations.comment: The conclusion synthesizes findings and connects them to urban planning implications, underlining the importance of green spaces in mitigating noise pollution.
annotations.groupName: Criterion E: Conclusion

annotations.comment: The screenshot noise map (Map 3) lacks a scale bar, legend and consistent symbology, making it difficult to interpret spatial data accurately.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

annotations.comment: Map 4 clearly marks Kasprowicza Parkâs area, helping to visualise site location, though further annotation (e.g., landmark labels) would enhance usability.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

annotations.comment: The hypotheses are clearly stated and directional, linking spatial and temporal expectations to the research question and enabling empirical testing.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

annotations.comment: For Hypothesis 1, consider quantifying the expected difference in dB between sites (e.g., â5â10 dB higherâ), which would make predictions more precise.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

annotations.comment: Ensure that the time periods defined in the hypothesis match exactly those used in data collection (e.g., morning: 8:00â12:00) for consistency.
annotations.groupName: Criterion B: Methods of Investigation

annotations.comment: The method description lacks detail on equipment calibration, replicate counts at each site, and control of external noise sources, reducing methodological transparency.
annotations.groupName: Criterion B: Methods of Investigation

annotations.comment: Recording decibel readings at regular five-minute intervals and calculating max/min/average values demonstrates a systematic approach and supports reproducibility.
annotations.groupName: Criterion B: Methods of Investigation

annotations.comment: To strengthen methodology, outline a sampling plan (e.g., number of transects or grid points per site) and justify how it captures spatial variability.
annotations.groupName: Criterion B: Methods of Investigation

annotations.comment: Collecting data only on a single day per site limits temporal representativeness; sampling over multiple days or seasons would increase data robustness.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

annotations.comment: The compiled table uses both commas and periods as decimal separators, which violates data convention consistency and may confuse interpretation.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

annotations.comment: When discussing summary statistics, include measures of variability (e.g., standard deviation or error bars) to reflect data spread and reliability.
annotations.groupName: Criterion D: Written Analysis

annotations.comment: The concise opening of the park analysis effectively transitions from mall to park, reinforcing thematic flow and linking back to the research question.
annotations.groupName: Criterion D: Written Analysis

annotations.comment: Graph 1 lacks axis labels with units and a legend to differentiate data series, reducing clarity in the visual presentation.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

annotations.comment: Analysis would be enriched by explicitly linking observed anomalies (e.g., evening mall noise) back to urban theory from Option G, deepening theoretical integration.
annotations.groupName: Criterion D: Written Analysis

annotations.comment: Quantify the noise reduction benefit of green spaces against WHO or EU noise guidelines in the conclusion to provide actionable context for urban planners.
annotations.groupName: Criterion E: Conclusion

annotations.comment: Ensure the bar graph for Kasprowicza Park uses the same color palette, axis scales and styling as the mall graph for direct visual comparison.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

annotations.comment: The evaluation identifies limitations but stops short of analysing how each (e.g., weather effects) specifically biases the dataset or results.
annotations.groupName: Criterion F: Evaluation

annotations.comment: For a more rigorous evaluation, outline a detailed plan for additional sampling (e.g., number of days, seasonal cycles) and explain how this would improve validity.
annotations.groupName: Criterion F: Evaluation

annotations.comment: The evaluation effectively acknowledges methodological strengths (consistent recording, site choice) and proposes future equipment upgrades, demonstrating reflective insight.
annotations.groupName: Criterion F: Evaluation
